name: John Kasich
summary: Former Ohio Governor and 2016 presidential candidate who became one of Trump's most prominent Republican critics, refusing to endorse him and speaking at the 2020 Democratic National Convention.

keyStatements.quote: I'm a Republican, but I am, first and foremost, an American. And right now, in our country, the most important thing we can do is choose the right leader.
keyStatements.context: Speaking at the 2020 Democratic National Convention endorsing Joe Biden

keyStatements.quote: I think that the path to the presidency is a path that people have to feel comfortable with. And when you stomp on people, when you say insulting things, it becomes very difficult.
He's just not fit to be president of the United States... The person who sits in the Oval Office must bring Americans together.

background: John Kasich served as Governor of Ohio from 2011 to 2019 and was a U.S. Representative from 1983 to 2001. He ran for president in 2016, positioning himself as a moderate alternative to Trump. Despite Trump winning the Republican nomination, Kasich refused to endorse him and skipped the 2016 Republican National Convention held in his home state.
oppositionReasons: Believes Trump's rhetoric is divisive and destructive to American unity
Opposes Trump's populist approach and personal attacks on opponents
Concerned about Trump's temperament and fitness for office
Disagrees with Trump's handling of racial tensions and protests
Believes Trump has damaged Republican Party values and conservatism
notableActions: Refused to endorse Trump in 2016 and 2020
Skipped the 2016 Republican National Convention in Cleveland, his home state
Spoke at the 2020 Democratic National Convention endorsing Joe Biden
Consistently criticized Trump throughout his presidency
Advocated for bipartisanship and moderation in politics

strengths: Two-term governor of crucial swing state Ohio
Reputation as pragmatic conservative willing to work across party lines
Successfully balanced Ohio's budget and expanded Medicaid
Maintained consistent principles despite political pressure
considerations: Some conservatives view him as too moderate or establishment
His 2016 presidential campaign gained little traction
Speaking at DNC alienated some Republican voters
